evals.name: basic-adoption
evals.prompt: I want to standardize all error responses in my NestJS API to RFC 9457 problem details. Set it up please.
assertions: package.json adds @camcima/nestjs-rfc9457 to dependencies
AppModule imports Rfc9457Module from @camcima/nestjs-rfc9457
Rfc9457Module.forRoot() is used (not forRootAsync — no env-driven config requested)
No typeBaseUri configured (user did not specify a base URI)
No catchAllExceptions: true (user did not request catching non-HttpException throwables)
No instanceStrategy other than default (user did not request traceability)
Does NOT manually register Rfc9457ExceptionFilter via APP_FILTER or app.useGlobalFilters
Does NOT install or import class-validator / class-transformer (not needed for this scope)
Does NOT install or import @nestjs/swagger (not requested)

evals.name: tier2-validation
evals.prompt: My NestJS app already uses class-validator DTOs and a global ValidationPipe with whitelist, forbidNonWhitelisted, and transform turned on. Validation errors come out as a flat array of strings. I want them structured with field path, constraints, and nested children for cases where I have @ValidateNested() — basically the rich format. Switch us over to RFC 9457 problem details and give us those structured errors.
assertions: package.json adds @camcima/nestjs-rfc9457 to dependencies
AppModule imports Rfc9457Module from @camcima/nestjs-rfc9457
main.ts uses createRfc9457ValidationPipeExceptionFactory() as the ValidationPipe exceptionFactory
main.ts preserves whitelist: true on the ValidationPipe options
main.ts preserves forbidNonWhitelisted: true on the ValidationPipe options
main.ts preserves transform: true on the ValidationPipe options
main.ts does NOT register a second ValidationPipe — the original is modified in place
Does NOT use validationExceptionMapper (Tier 1 path) — Tier 2 was the explicit ask

evals.name: swagger-and-problemtype
evals.prompt: We use @nestjs/swagger and we have two domain exceptions in src/exceptions/ — InsufficientFundsException and OrderNotFoundException. Wire RFC 9457 problem details with `https://api.example.com/problems` as the type base URI. Make those two exceptions produce stable problem-type URIs (insufficient-funds and order-not-found). And document the error responses in the OpenAPI spec under application/problem+json so frontend devs see the right shape.
assertions: package.json adds @camcima/nestjs-rfc9457 to dependencies
Rfc9457Module configured with typeBaseUri: 'https://api.example.com/problems'
AppModule imports DiscoveryModule from @nestjs/core
main.ts calls applyProblemDetailResponses(app) inside a lazy SwaggerModule.setup factory
applyProblemDetailResponses is imported from @camcima/nestjs-rfc9457/swagger
InsufficientFundsException has @ProblemType() with type ending in /insufficient-funds
OrderNotFoundException has @ProblemType() with type ending in /order-not-found
@ProblemType is imported from @camcima/nestjs-rfc9457 (not /swagger)
Each @ProblemType() also sets a title and a status (422 and 404 respectively)
